key_insight: Higher cold-spray pressure increases copper particle velocity beyond the bonding threshold, producing denser, more ductile deposits with better conductivity through stronger inter-particle bonding and microstructural refinement.
engineering_relevance: For CSAM copper parts, spray pressure is a key process lever to balance conductivity, ductility, and strength without melting the material.
method: Cold-sprayed copper at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar using a LightSPEE3D system
Characterised deposits with XRD, SEM, hardness, eddy-current conductivity, and tensile testing
Compared pressure-driven changes in particle velocity, microstructure, and mechanical/electrical performance
evidence_signal: The abstract reports higher-pressure deposits showed crystallite refinement, increased conductivity, lower hardness, and a clear shift toward ductile tensile behaviour with fully ductile fracture at the highest pressures.
watch_item: The optimum window appears pressure-dependent for this specific LightSPEE3D setup, so the same settings may not transfer directly to other nozzles, feedstocks, or stand-off conditions.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
